1.title: A prophet in Josiah's reign
1.text: Verse 1 dates Zephaniah's message to 'the days of Josiah,' the reforming king of Judah — a generation before Jerusalem fell to Babylon, while there was still time to turn back.

1.question: In Zephaniah 1:6, whom will the Lord cut off along with the idolaters?
options: Those turned back from the Lord who have not sought him

1.question: According to Zephaniah 1:10, from where shall there be the noise of a cry in that day?
In Zephaniah 1:12, how will the Lord search Jerusalem?

1.template: Neither their silver nor their {0} shall be able to deliver them

2.title: A refuge for the meek
2.text: Amid the warnings, verse 3 offers hope: the meek who 'seek righteousness, seek meekness' may be hidden in the day of the Lord's anger — judgment is not the only word.

2.question: In Zephaniah 2:4, which cities are named for desolation?

2.template: Surely Moab shall be as {0}, and the children of Ammon as Gomorrah

2.question: In Zephaniah 2:15, what had the rejoicing city said in her heart?
options: The Lord will not do evil
I am, and there is none beside me

3.question: How does Zephaniah 3:5 describe the Lord in the midst of the city?
options: He is just and will not do iniquity
He is silent and far off

3.question: In Zephaniah 3:9, what will the Lord turn to the people so that all may call on his name?
According to Zephaniah 3:15, what has the Lord done for the daughter of Zion?
options: Taken away her judgments and cast out her enemy

3.template: he will {0} over thee with joy

3.question: In Zephaniah 3:20, what does the Lord promise to make of his people among all the peoples of the earth?

3.title: God singing over his people
3.text: Verse 17 gives one of the tenderest images in scripture: the Lord 'will joy over thee with singing.' A book of judgment ends with God himself rejoicing over the restored remnant.
